abstract: MistTrack OpenAPI Toolkit—an AI-powered agent toolkit driven by the MistTrack Anti-Money Laundering (AML) tracking system. It supports cryptocurrency address risk scoring (AML/KYT/KYA), address tag lookup, address overviews, address profiling, transaction investigation, behavioral analysis, and counterparty analysis. The toolkit also includes multi-signature (multisig) address identification and permission analysis—detecting Gnosis Safe wallets on ETH/EVM chains, TRON native permission multisig, and Bitcoin P2SH/P2WSH/P2TR address formats. The toolkit supports over 200 tokens across more than 20 blockchains, including BTC, ETH, TRX, BNB, and SOL.

environment_variables.name: MISTTRACK_API_KEY
environment_variables.description: MistTrack API key for authenticating all API requests (recommended). All scripts prioritize this variable. If absent, x402 pay-per-use via scripts/pay.py --key-file is the alternative. Obtain at https://dashboard.misttrack.io/apikeys

payment_capability.trigger: --key-file <path> passed at invocation time; X402_PRIVATE_KEY env var is refused

runtime_enforcements.detail: $1.00 USDC hard cap enforced in build_payment_payload(); amounts above 1_000_000 units are rejected before signing

pay.py refuses to run if X402_PRIVATE_KEY is present in the environment; private keys must be supplied via --key-file <path> at invocation time — the key is read from a permission-restricted file and never appears on the command line
skills/payment.md sets disable_model_calls: true — advisory signal to agent platforms to block autonomous invocation; enforcement is harness-dependent, not guaranteed by this package

security_notes: Core AML scripts (risk_check, batch_risk_check, transfer_security_check, address_investigation, multisig_analysis) are read-only; they make GET/POST requests to openapi.misttrack.io only. scripts/pay.py can sign and broadcast on-chain USDC transactions when --key-file is supplied. Runtime code enforces: (1) $1.00 USDC per-call hard cap; (2) pay.py exits with an error if X402_PRIVATE_KEY is present in the environment. Private keys must be supplied via --key-file <path> at invocation time; the key is read from a permission-restricted file and never appears on the command line or in environment variables. X402_PRIVATE_KEY is not a supported input method. Recommended: use MISTTRACK_API_KEY for all normal usage.
